articles.summary: Vibe coding was supposed to make it easier to make cool stuff. But without security guardrails, it often turns into a big mess.

articles.title: EARLY LOOK SHOWS APPLE'S LIQUID GLASS-STYLE BLUR EFFECTS COMING TO ANDROID 17
articles.summary: Google is testing blur effects in Android 17 that resemble Apple's Liquid Glass design, with translucent backgrounds appearing in system interfaces like the volume panel. The frosted visuals are tied to new UI flags and create a layered appearance in which content appears to float above blurred backgrounds. The feature remains under development and may change before Android 17's final release, alongside other updates, including private contact sharing and motion sickness reduction tools.

articles.title: GOOGLE PHOTOS BRINGS PROMPT EDITING TO INDIA, AUSTRALIA, AND JAPAN
articles.summary: Google is expanding prompt-based AI editing in Google Photos to India, Australia, and Japan, letting users make natural-language edits on everyday photos—on-device, across many Android phones, and in multiple local languages—while adding C2PA credentials for transparent AI attribution. The move matters because it brings fast, private, conversational editing to huge mobile audiences, positioning Google against Adobe, Apple, and Samsung with a simpler, more accessible workflow despite some early limits with complex scenes.

articles.title: LEAKER MAKES UNLIKELY CLAIM ABOUT IMPROVED IPHONE 18 TELEPHOTO PERFORMANCE
articles.summary: A leaker claims Apple is testing variable-aperture lenses and even teleconverters for the iPhone 18's telephoto camera, but the report is vague and widely seen as unlikely. While variable apertures could offer slightly better depth-of-field control on Pro models, the teleconverter claim is probably a mistranslation or misunderstanding rather than a real feature.

articles.title: HOW TOP COMPANIES ARE USING AI IN THEIR DESIGN WORKFLOWS
articles.summary: Leading tech companies are implementing structured AI workflows in design: Atlassian uses pre-built templates and instruction files to control AI prototyping, while Meta designers automate execution tasks while maintaining manual processes for research and strategy. Teams at Tesco are building custom Figma plugins that connect to live website data, and Faire deployed an internal AI chatbot called Fairey to analyze user queries and synthesize research interviews efficiently.

articles.title: ON CODING AGENTS AND THE FUTURE OF DESIGN
articles.summary: Coding agents like Claude Code represent a new phase of responsive design by stripping applications down to their essential primitives. This shift forces organizations to clarify what their products actually do, much like mobile-first design revealed that simpler interfaces often better serve user needs. This agent-driven future elevates design to pure strategy, where competitive advantage comes from honestly expressing capabilities rather than cluttering interfaces with organizational complexity.

articles.title: IS THE DOOMSDAY CLOCK THE MOST IMPORTANT DESIGN OF OUR TIME?
articles.summary: The Doomsday Clock, now set at 85 seconds to midnight, endures as one of the most powerful pieces of graphic design ever made, distilling complex existential threats—nuclear war, climate change, and AI risk—into a simple, universally understood metaphor. Though subjective and imprecise, its strength lies in provoking awareness and urgency, proving that design's impact isn't accuracy but its ability to focus global attention and spur debate.

articles.title: IT GETS LONELY WAITING IN BACKLOG
articles.summary: You designed a great prototype…three months ago. Now it's wasting away in backlog and you're wondering why you even tried. With v0 [4], every prompt generates production-ready code in a real git repository - so your frontend engineering friends have no more excuses. Start

articles.title: RESEARCH TOOL MADE EASY
articles.summary: PingPolls makes surveys simple, beautiful, and powerful with AI that helps you design, distribute, and analyze responses.

articles.title: DESCRIBE AND BUILD A DEVICE
articles.summary: Generates a parts list, printable enclosures, firmware, and assembly steps from a text description.

articles.title: GOOD WEBSITES
articles.summary: A curated collection of inspirational good websites.

articles.title: I LEARNED THE FIRST RULE OF ARIA THE HARD WAY
articles.summary: A developer added role="link" to a button element, intending to style it differently, which broke keyboard activation and caused screen readers to announce conflicting roles, even though it passed automated accessibility tests. Removing the ARIA attribute and using a CSS class instead restored proper functionality, demonstrating that semantic HTML elements already provide necessary accessibility features. ARIA should only supplement native elements to convey dynamic states, such as aria-expanded, rather than override their inherent roles and behaviors.

articles.title: STILLS 2026 TRENDS REPORT POINTS TO THE RISE OF HUMAN-CENTRED DESIGN
articles.summary: In a sea of AI-polished sameness, Stills' 2026 Photography in Design Trend Report argues that work cuts through by embracing play, imperfection, texture, and risk—making humanity visible again through colour, detail, and intentional weirdness. Across trends from scrapbook aesthetics and cyber goth to maximalism, grunge, surrealism, and sensory storytelling, the message is clear: audiences crave bold, tactile, emotionally resonant work that feels made by people, not machines.

articles.title: NEW LAUNDRY BRAND EAT DIRT PRIMED TO SHAKE UP THE ECO-FRIENDLY MARKET
articles.summary: Former ad execs Jordan Woolley and Catherine Barr have launched Eat Dirt, a plant-based, biodegradable laundry detergent positioned as an eco-friendly yet design-forward alternative, packaged in bold, illustrated tins meant to be seen rather than hidden. Aiming at environmentally and design-conscious consumers, the brand uses irreverent naming, playful illustration, and premium pricing to shake up a conservative category and reach buyers beyond traditional supermarket channels.

articles.title: DO RECRUITERS REALLY CARE ABOUT THE RESUME DESIGN?
articles.summary: Resume design significantly impacts recruiter perception, as messy layouts signal poor attention to detail and prompt immediate rejection during the critical 6-second initial scan window.

articles.title: TASTE WILL BE THE NEW CREATIVE SUPERPOWER IN 2026
articles.summary: Taste and human discernment will become the defining creative differentiators in 2026 as AI-generated content floods digital spaces with low-quality "slop."

articles.title: COLE PALMER'S NEW LOGO HAS A GENIUS DOUBLE MEANING
articles.summary: Footballer Cole Palmer has unveiled a minimalist new logo that interlocks his initials to mimic his iconic crossed-arms “Ice Man” celebration.
